cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what advice Wesla Whitfield would most likely give to other disabled people, based on the story provided. She struggled with depression and even tried to kill herself, but eventually recovered. She said that she realized she hadn't lost everything—the only thing she lost was the ability to walk. She emphasized that her mind was still her most valuable asset and said, "Everything important is in here. The only real disability in life is losing your mind."
